initiative: SEC chair Atkins signals new rules for onchain markets, AI-driven finance
description: SEC Chair Paul Atkins used a public appearance at the AI+ Expo in Washington to formally signal that the agency intends to pursue notice-and-comment rulemaking — rather than continued enforcement actions — to address how existing securities statutes apply to blockchain-based trading, settlement infrastructure, crypto vaults, and AI-driven financial applications. The core regulatory problem he identified is structural: securities law was designed around discrete intermediaries such as brokers, exchanges, and clearinghouses, whereas modern blockchain protocols can bundle all of those functions into a single piece of software, creating ambiguity about which rules apply and to whom.

initiative: Ripple shares North Korea-linked threat data as crypto attacks shift to social engineering
description: Ripple has contributed its proprietary threat intelligence — including fraud-associated domains, wallet addresses, and indicators of compromise tied to North Korean hacking campaigns — to Crypto ISAC, a sector-focused information-sharing nonprofit, which simultaneously launched a new API enabling real-time data distribution among members including Coinbase. The disclosure is framed around a documented tactical shift by North Korea-linked actors away from on-chain exploits toward insider-style social engineering, as illustrated by the $280 million Drift incident, where attackers gained the trust of contributors and compromised their devices rather than targeting smart contract vulnerabilities.

initiative: US spot bitcoin ETFs draw $532 million as inflows extend to a third day, led by BlackRock and Fidelity

BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC dominated activity, contributing $335.5 million and $184.6 million respectively, with Morgan Stanley's MSBT adding a further $12.2 million — the remaining ten funds, including Grayscale's GBTC, reported flat flows and zero outflows.

initiative: Tiff Macklem: Release of the Monetary Policy Report
description: Bank of Canada Holds Rate at 2.25% Amid Middle East-Driven Energy Shock At its April 29, 2026 decision, the Bank of Canada's Governing Council held the overnight policy rate steady at 2.25% — unchanged since October 2025 — as policymakers weighed competing pressures from an energy-driven inflation spike against softening domestic demand and trade headwinds from U.S. tariffs. The escalation of the Middle East conflict has driven global oil prices to approximately USD 90/barrel, pushing Canadian CPI from 1.8% in February to 2.4% in March, with the Bank projecting a peak near 3% in April before a return to the 2% target by early 2026, contingent on oil prices declining to around USD 75/barrel.

initiative: Aleš Michl: A central bank. And bitcoin.
description: Czech National Bank Governor Aleš Michl delivered a formal speech at The Bitcoin Conference 2026 in Las Vegas, marking a notable instance of a sitting G10-adjacent central bank governor directly engaging a crypto-native audience at an industry event. The speech framed the CNB's post-2022 monetary tightening—which successfully reduced inflation from near 20% to the 2% target—as a philosophical foundation for evaluating Bitcoin alongside traditional reserve management principles. Michl positioned the CNB's approach as structurally hawkish and pro-saving, implicitly signaling openness to hard-asset or scarcity-based monetary concepts consistent with that framework.

initiative: Jorgovanka Tabaković: Brave old world

The speech used the report's demographic focus as a platform to articulate the NBS's institutional view that declining fertility, labour force contraction, and population ageing represent primary structural risks to GDP growth, fiscal sustainability, and inflation dynamics across both Serbia and the broader European transition region.

None of the seven platforms hold SEC registration or authorization, exposing them and any local promoters to penalties under the Securities Regulation Code of up to PHP 5 million (approximately USD 89,000) and up to 21 years' imprisonment.

initiative: Tether Proposes Three-Way Merger to Build Integrated Bitcoin Platform Around Twenty One Capital
description: Tether Investments, the largest shareholder of NYSE-listed Twenty One Capital (ticker: XXI), has formally proposed merging XXI with Strike—the bitcoin payments and financial services firm led by Jack Mallers—and Elektron Energy, a bitcoin mining operator controlling roughly 50 EH/s (approximately 5% of total network hashrate) at sub-$60,000 all-in production costs. The transaction would consolidate a bitcoin treasury vehicle, a retail and institutional lending/payments business, and a large-scale mining operation into a single publicly listed entity, with Tether signaling it will vote its controlling stake in favor of both deals.

initiative: Shifting forces behind RMB internationalization: evidence from the 2025 Triennial Survey
description: Using granular data from the 2025 BIS Triennial Survey on global FX turnover — including previously restricted BIS locational banking statistics and international debt securities data — researchers find that cross-border banking linkages with China, rather than bilateral trade flows, are the primary short-term driver of RMB FX trading growth across jurisdictions. Policy-driven mechanisms such as qualified investor licence programmes and free trade agreements emerge as significant long-term structural determinants, though their effects materialise slowly beyond the three-year survey interval.

initiative: Digitalisation and innovation - opportunities and risks for financial health
description: BIS FSI Brief: Digitalisation, Innovation, and Financial Health Risks The Bank for International Settlements' Financial Stability Institute published a 22-page brief examining how digital financial services — spanning payments, credit, savings, and insurance — are simultaneously improving access and generating new consumer harm vectors, including a documented global surge in scams and fraud, overindebtedness among digital borrowers, and mis-selling of complex investment products. Despite broader digital adoption, aggregate financial health indices are declining in several jurisdictions, undermining the assumed positive correlation between fintech penetration and household financial resilience.

initiative: Scope of Insurers Subject to the Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements in the FSB Key Attributes: Final Report
description: The Financial Stability Board has published its final guidance establishing a structured framework for national authorities to determine which insurers must be subject to recovery and resolution planning requirements under the FSB's Key Attributes of Effective Resolution Regimes. The report follows the FSB's 2022 decision to discontinue the annual designation of global systemically important insurers, replacing that binary G-SII identification process with a more flexible, criteria-based scoping methodology that individual jurisdictions apply using their own supervisory judgment and market context.

initiative: Scope of Insurers Subject to Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements in the Key Attributes: Overview of consultation responses
description: The FSB received nine responses to its November 2025 consultation on criteria for determining which insurers should be subject to recovery and resolution planning requirements under the Key Attributes framework, with the consultation period closing in February 2026. Respondents — drawn from both public authorities and private sector participants — broadly supported the proposed scoping criteria but flagged concerns around regulatory overreach and potential duplication with existing national or sectoral frameworks. The FSB has now published both an overview of those responses and a final report incorporating targeted revisions to address the feedback, alongside updated guidance on identifying critical functions for insurers.

initiative: Identification of Critical Functions of Insurers: Practices paper – Revised version
description: The Financial Stability Board has released a revised version of its 2023 practices paper on identifying critical functions of insurers, updated to reflect a new definition of "critical function" introduced in the FSB's concurrently finalised guidance on the scope of insurers subject to recovery and resolution planning under the Key Attributes framework. The revision draws on approaches from four jurisdictions — Australia, China, France, and the Netherlands — documenting how each has developed or advanced methodology for scoping which insurer functions are sufficiently vital to the real economy that their disruption would warrant resolution intervention.

initiative: Key Attributes Assessment Methodology for the Insurance Sector: Revised version
description: The Financial Stability Board published a revised version of its Key Attributes Assessment Methodology for the Insurance Sector on 29 April 2026, updating the 2020 original to incorporate changes stemming from newly finalized guidance on which insurers fall within the scope of recovery and resolution planning requirements. The revision reflects the FSB's 2022 decision—reaffirmed in November 2025—to discontinue annual identification of global systemically important insurers (G-SIIs) and instead rely on the IAIS Holistic Framework to assess systemic risk, a structural shift that necessitated an updated definition of "critical function" embedded in the methodology via an explanatory note.

initiative: Developing Effective Resolution Strategies and Plans for Systemically Important Insurers: Revised version
description: The Financial Stability Board has released a revised version of its 2016 guidance on resolution strategies and plans for systemically important insurers, updated to incorporate changes to the definition of "critical functions" introduced through a companion scoping paper finalized simultaneously. The revised guidance is directed at national resolution authorities and Crisis Management Groups overseeing global systemically important insurers, setting out how preferred resolution strategies should be determined by analyzing business models, policyholder protection arrangements, and the criticality of specific insurance functions.

initiative: Singapore proposes lower-risk rules for some digital assets

initiative: Australia’s Crypto Crossroads: Regulation is Here, Now Comes the Hard Part

Stablecoin oversight and scam prevention are being developed on separate policy tracks — stablecoins through payments reform legislation, scams through the Scam Prevention Framework targeting banks, telcos, and social media platforms.

description: For EU-regulated CASPs, the designation of a Kyrgyz A7A5 exchange, the focus on Russia-adjacent financial institutions, and the first use of the anti-circumvention tool are all strong signals that supervisors will expect sanctions-evasion risk to be built into AMLR-driven, harmonized CDD/EDD frameworks by the time the rules bite.
