initiative: Cryptoasset service providers as financial intermediaries: risks and policy approaches
description: The largest firms now offer a broad range of products, including yield/earn programmes, margin and secured lending, derivatives and token issuance. Some of these products closely resemble financial intermediation activities traditionally performed by banks and prime brokers.

initiative: Mastercard Strengthens Sri Lanka’s Digital Payments Ecosystem
description: Hey Payments Fanatic! Mastercard is doubling down on Sri Lanka, and small merchants are the big winners. With the launch of Soundbox and Cloud Commerce , the card network is making digital payments cheaper, simpler, and far more accessible. Soundbox is a first in the market: a compact device that gives

initiative: How Thought Leadership Insights Can Be a Flywheel for Investor Trust
description: In this Finextra TV interview from the Communify Intelligence Experience in Miami, Pam Finelli, Global COO, Investment Research, Deutsche Bank, explored the importance of personalised advice to clients, allowing consistency on the back end. Finelli explains research helps banks use content as a flywheel to unlock opportunities with clients, utilising thought leadership insights to bolster trust and respect as a

initiative: Kalshi flags more insider trading cases, including politician who appeared on FBoy Island
description: Kalshi, the CFTC-regulated prediction market exchange, publicly disclosed three additional insider trading enforcement actions against political candidates who placed bets on their own electoral outcomes — a practice the platform classifies as a direct violation of its rulebook given candidates' ability to influence contract results by choosing whether to enter or exit a race. The cases involved a Virginia U.S. Senate candidate, a Minnesota state lawmaker running for Congress, and a Texas congressional candidate, with penalties ranging from $540 to $6,229 in fines plus five-year trading suspensions and profit disgorgement. Two cooperated and received reduced penalties, while the Virginia candidate publicly defied the process and received the stiffest sanction.

initiative: SEC confirms exemption for directors and officers of EEA Foreign Private Issuers
description: The SEC has granted an exemption relieving directors and officers of EEA-domiciled foreign private issuers from the beneficial ownership and transaction reporting obligations under Section 16(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, which would otherwise require prompt disclosure of changes in holdings of registered equity securities. The decision reflects a regulatory equivalence determination, with the SEC acknowledging that EU Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) already imposes materially comparable disclosure obligations on persons discharging managerial responsibilities at listed companies.

initiative: Banks seek to slow down implementation of crypto's GENIUS Act on stablecoin oversight
description: U.S. banking groups argued that a number of federal agencies are moving quickly on stablecoin regulations, making it hard to understand how rules will interact.

initiative: Bitcoin tops $79,000 as crypto rally gathers steam; Circle, Coinbase, Strategy lead
description: Bitcoin reached an 11-week high above $79,000 on April 22, driven by a combination of improving macro sentiment following President Trump's Iran ceasefire extension and technical positioning dynamics in derivatives markets. Perpetual swap funding rates fell to near three-year lows while open interest simultaneously rose, a configuration K33 Research's Vetle Lunde flagged as indicative of concentrated short buildup in leveraged positions — conditions that historically precede a mechanically driven short squeeze. Crypto-linked equities moved sharply in sympathy, with Strategy gaining 10% as its BTC-heavy balance sheet amplified the move, while Circle Internet and Coinbase rose 9% and 6% respectively ahead of Circle's anticipated public listing.

initiative: TD Cowen flags five hurdles to crypto bill beyond stablecoin yield issue
description: These include a lack of CFTC commissioners, conflicts tied to World Liberty Financial, and concerns around Iran's use of crypto payments.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of First Financial Bank
description: Federal Reserve Enforcement Action Against Former First Financial Bank Employee The Federal Reserve Board issued a consent prohibition order on April 22, 2026, against Destiny Lara, a former employee of First Financial Bank, headquartered in Abilene, Texas. The action stems from findings of breach of fiduciary duty and bribery, two serious violations that typically indicate insider misconduct involving the misuse of a position of trust for personal gain. Consent prohibition orders bar the named individual from participating in the affairs of any federally regulated financial institution, making this a career-ending sanction in the banking sector.

initiative: The London P2P sweep: UK FCA raids eight illegal peer-to-peer trading hubs
description: The FCA, operating jointly with HMRC and the South West Regional Organised Crime Unit, executed simultaneous raids across eight London locations suspected of running unregistered crypto peer-to-peer trading operations — marking the regulator's first coordinated enforcement action of this type. Each site received a cease-and-desist notice, and seized evidence is now underpinning multiple active criminal investigations focused on money laundering exposure. The action carries particular regulatory weight given that the FCA has confirmed zero registered P2P crypto traders currently exist in the UK, meaning all such operations are by definition unlawful under the existing AML registration framework.

initiative: Crypto giant GSR launches its first ETF to give investors an easy way to bet on the big 3 tokens
description: GSR, a firm best known as a crypto liquidity provider and OTC market maker, has entered the asset management space by listing the GSR Crypto Core3 ETF (BESO) on Nasdaq—its first registered investment product. The actively managed fund holds bitcoin, ether, and solana, rebalances weekly using proprietary research signals, and can generate staking yield on eligible positions, with Framework Digital Advisors serving as the named investment adviser and a 1% annual management fee applying. The structure is notable because it combines active allocation with staking income in a single wrapper, features that have only recently become permissible in U.S.-listed crypto ETFs following regulatory shifts that allowed in-kind treatment and yield-bearing mechanics.

initiative: UK FCA carries out first crackdown on illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading
description: The FCA conducted coordinated physical inspections of eight London premises suspected of operating unregistered peer-to-peer crypto trading activity, working alongside HM Revenue & Customs and the South West Regional Organised Crime Unit. Cease-and-desist letters were issued at each site, and evidence gathered is now feeding into active criminal investigations — a notable escalation given that the regulator has confirmed zero FCA-registered peer-to-peer crypto traders or platforms currently exist in the UK. The action is grounded in existing AML registration requirements that already apply to certain crypto activities ahead of the broader regulatory regime expected to go live in October 2027, meaning firms cannot defer compliance on the basis that the full framework is incomplete.

initiative: Petra Tschudin, Thomas Moser: Swipe, scan, tap - the cashless payments market
description: Two members of the Swiss National Bank's Governing Board delivered a structured public address at the Zurich Money Market Event examining the architecture and economics of Switzerland's cashless payments ecosystem. The speech drew on SNB household payment survey data showing cash now accounts for roughly 30% of point-of-sale transactions, cards approximately 50%, and mobile payment apps—which have existed for only a decade—around 20% and growing fastest. The presentation was framed explicitly around the SNB's institutional mandate to maintain an efficient and resilient national payments infrastructure, signalling the central bank's active monitoring posture toward the structural shift away from cash.

initiative: Erik Thedéen: Vulnerabilities and resilience in a new world order
description: Sveriges Riksbank Governor Erik Thedéen used a Stockholm security conference to outline concrete operational steps the Riksbank is taking to harden Sweden's payment infrastructure against geopolitical and physical disruption risks. Most notably, from 1 July 2026 the Riksbank will mandate offline card payment capability for essential goods purchases in Sweden, and has launched a formal investigation into enabling offline functionality for Swish, the dominant mobile instant-payment platform. Separately, the Swedish government has proposed requiring staffed grocery stores and pharmacies to accept cash from the same date, a measure Thedéen explicitly endorsed as a contingency backstop.

initiative: Paolo Angelini: DLT and stablecoins - where do we stand?
description: Speech by Mr Paolo Angelini, Senior Deputy Governor of the Bank of Italy, at the International Economic Symposium "The global economy at an inflection point: technology, policy, and the future of growth", co-hosted by the Bank of Italy and National Association for Business Economics (NABE), Rome, 20 April 2026.

initiative: ESMA sets out actions to simplify the retail investor journey and make investing more accessible
description: ESMA Publishes Retail Investor Journey Reform Agenda Following 2025 Stakeholder Consultation Following its 2025 Call for Evidence, ESMA has published a formal report synthesising stakeholder feedback and committing to concrete follow-up work across three operational areas: condensing and digitalising product disclosures, simplifying MiFID II suitability and appropriateness assessment frameworks, and reducing complexity in sustainability preference integration. The authority will deploy consumer testing—including mobile-first user research—to validate proposed changes to disclosure design and digital onboarding flows before translating findings into technical advice on MiFID II delegated acts and potential guideline revisions.

initiative: B2B stablecoin tech provider Infinite rolls out banking services powered by Thiel-backed Erebor Bank
description: Infinite is introducing bank accounts that support traditional and crypto services like ACH, wire and stablecoin transfers.

initiative: Onchain vault provider Upshift taps Securitize Fund Services for third-party reporting
description: Upshift is tapping Securitize Fund Services to provide independent reporting, auditing, and performance transparency for its onchain vaults.

initiative: EU financial markets enter 2026 amid high-risk environment
description: ESMA's first Trends, Risks and Vulnerabilities report of 2026 assessed EU market conditions through the second half of 2025, concluding that systemic and market stress risks remain elevated despite broadly resilient performance during that period. The assessment was finalized before a late-February Middle East conflict escalation, yet early market reactions — including sharp energy and commodity price increases and heightened volatility — validated the transmission channels ESMA had already flagged, underscoring the report's operational relevance.

initiative: Investment funds' de facto currency risk exposure
description: Investment Funds' De Facto Currency Risk Exposure A BIS research bulletin published in April 2026 introduces a return-sensitivity framework to measure the actual, rather than declared, currency hedge ratios of investment funds, finding materially different behaviour between asset classes. Bond funds maintain consistently high hedge ratios, though these show some responsiveness to the cost of hedging, while equity funds exhibit volatile, opportunistic positioning that functionally resembles active currency speculation rather than systematic risk management.

initiative: Stablecoins are becoming the rails for 24/7 global trade
description: Hong Kong's HKMA issues stablecoin licenses, signaling a shift in digital assets towards a regulated financial system driven by major banks.

initiative: Fed pick Warsh backs crypto’s place in finance as Warren raises ‘sock puppet’ concerns in hearing
description: Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee to chair the Federal Reserve, appeared before the Senate Banking Committee for his confirmation hearing and affirmed that digital assets are already embedded in the U.S. financial system, signaling a materially more accommodative posture toward crypto at the Fed than under current Chair Jerome Powell.

initiative: Crypto's massive exploit may force big banks to rethink their blockchain plans, Jefferies warns
description: The $293 million Kelp DAO exploit has exposed critical infrastructure risks, leading Jefferies to suggest that traditional financial firms may pause their blockchain initiatives to prioritize security.

initiative: Bitcoin slides toward $75,000 as Warsh says Trump didn't demand he cut rates
description: During Kevin Warsh's Senate Banking Committee confirmation hearing, the Fed chair nominee explicitly denied coordinating rate positions with President Trump and emphasized the Federal Reserve's institutional independence, dampening market expectations for near-term easing that had been partly priced into risk assets including bitcoin. Simultaneously, geopolitical risk repriced higher as U.S.-Iran ceasefire negotiations approached a Wednesday deadline with reports of stalled talks, a halted Vance diplomatic trip, and fresh Treasury-linked sanctions targeting 14 individuals and entities involved in weapons procurement for Iran.

initiative: ESMA releases reporting templates and instructions for the Active Account Requirement
description: ESMA has published standardised reporting templates and accompanying instructions that firms subject to the Active Account Requirement under EMIR 3 must use when disclosing AAR-related data to their national competent authorities. The AAR, which became applicable on 25 June 2025, obliges certain financial counterparties to maintain operationally active clearing accounts at EU-based CCPs for specified derivatives categories, and the new templates define precisely how compliance with that obligation must be evidenced and structured. The first mandatory submission is due 31 July 2026 and will cover the roughly twelve-month period since the requirement took effect, with semi-annual reporting cycles on fixed January and July deadlines thereafter.

initiative: Bipartisan PACE Act looks to open Fed payment rails to nonbanks, draws crypto support
description: Introduced by California Democratic Representative Sam Liccardo and Republican Representative Young Kim, the Payments Access and Consumer Efficiency (PACE) Act would establish an optional federal supervisory framework administered by the OCC, under which qualifying nonbank payment service providers — currently forced to navigate up to 40 separate state money transmitter licenses — could register at the federal level and gain direct access to Federal Reserve infrastructure including Fedwire, FedNow, and FedACH. Eligible firms would be required to hold 1:1 reserves against customer balances and comply with standardized risk and recordkeeping requirements.

initiative: Bitget exchange brings pre-IPO tokens to masses starting with SpaceX on Solana
description: Bitget launched IPO Prime in partnership with Republic, a regulated private-markets investment platform, to offer retail crypto users tokenized exposure to pre-IPO companies via Solana-based derivatives. The initial instrument, preSPAX, tracks SpaceX's implied valuation ahead of its anticipated public listing, with users committing stablecoins into a pooled subscription that converts into tradeable tokens upon distribution. Critically, the tokens carry no equity ownership rights and function as valuation-linked derivatives rather than securities, a structural distinction with significant regulatory implications as the SEC continues to develop frameworks for onchain capital markets.

initiative: UK invites crypto giant Bybit to London to win over some of UAE’s innovation shine
description: UK government-linked economic development officials formally invited Bybit CEO Ben Zhou to London during UK Fintech Week, arranging meetings with the Financial Conduct Authority and members of the House of Lords to discuss the country's forthcoming crypto regulatory framework and potential establishment of a Bybit presence in the jurisdiction. The outreach is explicitly framed around competitive positioning against the UAE, which has attracted both Bybit—headquartered in Dubai since 2022—and Binance, which relocated there in 2025, creating a cluster effect that drew smaller crypto firms to the region.

initiative: European banks are at risk of losing customers to rivals with better crypto tools
description: A Boerse Stuttgart Digital survey of 6,000 retail investors across Germany, Italy, Spain, and France—conducted between August 2025 and January 2026—found that 35% would consider switching their primary bank to one offering stronger crypto investment services, with Spain leading at 40%. Approximately 25% of respondents already hold digital assets, yet over 60% report feeling poorly informed and 76% cite insufficient regulation as a primary risk concern, underscoring a gap between adoption and confidence. Critically, surveyed investors were more than twice as likely to trust their incumbent bank for crypto custody and trading than a specialist platform, meaning traditional institutions hold a structural trust advantage they have so far failed to monetize.

initiative: DoorDash is bringing stablecoin payments to masses with Stripe-backed blockchain
description: The delivery service is working with the Stripe-led blockchain firm Tempo to bring stablecoin payouts to its global marketplace, replacing fragmented regional rails.

initiative: Circle tops crypto stocks with 30% gain as stablecoin growth outpaces Coinbase slump
description: Every crypto-related stock in the market now has its own differentiated business models that respond to different drivers.

initiative: DoorDash to offer stablecoin payouts with Tempo in push toward everyday crypto payments
description: Stripe, Coastal Bank and ARQ are also deploying stablecoin payment flows on Tempo's network alongside DoorDash.

initiative: Björk Sigurgísladóttir: Financial supervision from the perspective of the Deputy Governor for Financial Supervision
description: At the Central Bank of Iceland's Annual Financial Supervision Day on 19 March 2026, Deputy Governor Björk Sigurgísladóttir outlined the Bank's 2025 supervisory activities and forward priorities, coinciding with publication of the Bank's annual Financial Supervision report. Key operational disclosures included results from a thematic sanctions-screening check conducted across fourteen obliged entities using simulated data and foreign expert assistance, with individual feedback issued and aggregate findings published in December 2025, marking a methodological departure from prior supervisory approaches.

initiative: Joachim Nagel: Already here, not yet everywhere - shaping the economic impact of artificial intelligence
description: Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel delivered a keynote address at the International Economic Symposium in Rome on 21 April 2026, setting out his institutional view on how artificial intelligence is likely to reshape growth, inflation dynamics, and financial stability — the three core variables central banks monitor for policy calibration. He drew an explicit parallel to the slow-burn productivity diffusion seen during electrification, while arguing AI may propagate faster than prior general-purpose technologies, implying a potentially compressed window for policymakers to assess and respond to structural economic shifts.

initiative: Anna Breman: Global shockwaves to Kiwi shores - the impact of the Iran conflict on New Zealand
description: Reserve Bank of New Zealand Governor Anna Breman used a keynote address to Business NZ's CEO Forum in Auckland to formally communicate the central bank's assessment of how the ongoing Iran-related Middle East conflict is transmitting into New Zealand's macroeconomic and financial conditions. She flagged that near-term headline inflation is likely to rise while growth momentum softens, though core inflation has held at 2.4% and medium-term expectations remain anchored—circumstances that constrain but do not preclude further OCR adjustments from the current 2.25% level.

initiative: Fabio Panetta: Statement - meeting of the Development Committee
description: Labor market regulation should support firm growth while preserving incentives to invest in worker training and ensuring adequate contractual protection. Beyond commendable efforts already under way to develop an effective originate-to-distribute model, the IFC 2030 strategy should deepen upstream engagement and prioritize projects with strong market-creation impact and clear additionality. Fabio Panetta: Statement meeting of the Development Committee

initiative: ESMA support ESEF implementation with updated taxonomy
description: ESMA published the 2025 ESEF XBRL taxonomy files and an updated Conformance Suite on 21 April 2026, providing the technical infrastructure EU-listed issuers and their software vendors need to prepare IFRS consolidated financial statements in structured electronic format for the 2026 reporting cycle. The key substantive change embedded in the taxonomy is the incorporation of IFRS 18 Presentation and Disclosure in Financial Statements — mandatory from 1 January 2027 but permitting early adoption — with two separate entry points allowing filers to report under either the existing IAS 1 framework or the incoming IFRS 18 standard without requiring an RTS amendment.

initiative: Securitize names former IMF representative to board
description: Sunil Sabharwal served as a Senate-confirmed U.S. representative to the International Monetary Fund between 2016 and 2018.

initiative: UK FCA opens consultation on crypto scope
description: The U.K.'s FCA is seekign feedback on its digital currency regulation, defining qualifying cryptoassets and stablecoins, ahead of new rules taking effect in October 2027.

initiative: Bank of Korea’s new governor signals CBDC and bank token push, skips stablecoins in key address
description: The BOK will increase scrutiny of crypto markets and non-bank finance, and will also modernize currency markets for 24-hour foreign exchange trading, he said.

initiative: Ripple Unveils Four-Phase Roadmap to Make the XRP Ledger Quantum-Resistant by 2028
description: Ripple's four-phase plan targets full post-quantum cryptography on the XRP Ledger by 2028, with a Quantum-Day contingency already in place and NIST-standard testing underway in 2026.

initiative: Strategy overtakes BlackRock IBIT in bitcoin holdings after bear market buying
description: Strategy Reclaims Top Bitcoin Holder Position from BlackRock IBIT Strategy has accumulated approximately 80,000 BTC in 2026 alone — largely during a period when bitcoin fell more than 50% from its October all-time high — pushing its total holdings to 815,061 BTC and edging past BlackRock's IBIT, which holds 802,824 BTC. The accumulation was funded primarily through a combination of at-the-market equity issuance, convertible debt, and perpetual preferred securities (STRC), the last of which has become a scalable, recurring capital source that distinguishes Strategy's model from passive ETF structures.

initiative: ECB backs centralized oversight of major crypto firms
description: The European Central Bank published a formal opinion on April 9 endorsing the European Commission's December 2025 "Market Integration and Supervision" package, which proposes transferring authorization, monitoring, and enforcement powers over crypto-asset service providers (CASPs) from national competent authorities (NCAs) to ESMA. The move responds to documented inconsistencies in how member states — particularly smaller jurisdictions such as Malta and Luxembourg that became popular MiCA licensing hubs — have applied the regulation, with France's AMF characterizing the dynamic as a regulatory race to the bottom and threatening to block firms licensed elsewhere.

initiative: A dozen banks want a euro stablecoin. Fireblocks is making it happen
description: The Qivalis consortium is made up of: Banca Sella, BBVA, BNP Paribas, CaixaBank, Danske Bank, DekaBank, DZ BANK, ING, KBC, Raiffeisen Bank International, SEB, and UniCredit.

initiative: Japan’s JSCC partners with Mizuho, Nomura to test government bonds on blockchain
description: The proof-of-concept trial will test the onchain transfer and management of Japanese government bonds on Canton Network.

initiative: ElectrumSVP v0.1.0 launches, with self-custody security upgrades
description: ElectrumSVP v0.1.0 brings advanced self-custody tools for BSV users, enhancing transaction handling, fee logic, and UTXO management.

initiative: Bank of Korea’s new chief vows to push CBDC, deposit tokens; leaves out stablecoins
description: BOK Governor Shin Hyun-song previously took a negative stance on stablecoins during his tenure at the BIS.

initiative: Nomura, Mizuho, JSCC to trial tokenized collateral on Canton Network
description: Today Nomura, Mizuho and the Japan Securities Clearing Corporation (JSCC) and Digital Asset Holdings unveiled plans for a tokenized collateral trial for Japanese government bonds (JGBs) on the Canton Network. Two fo the proof of concept (PoC) participants, Nomura and Mizuho, were previously announced as participants of an FSA sandbox, the Payment Innovation Project (PIP)

initiative: ESMA clarifies expectations in the run-up to the launch of EU’s Consolidated Tapes
description: ESMA published a Q&A document addressing the onboarding process for data contributors and operational rules governing the two selected Consolidated Tape Providers — fairCT for bonds and EuroCTP for equities — ahead of their formal authorisation. Trading venues and Authorised Publication Arrangements are reminded of their legal obligation under MiFIR to feed data into the CTs from go-live, and ESMA is pressing these contributors to begin technical engagement with the CTPs now, including agreeing on transmission protocols and completing connectivity and end-to-end testing. CTPs are simultaneously required to implement confidentiality and integrity safeguards for any data received during this pre-authorisation preparatory phase.

initiative: Grayscale’s amended Hyperliquid ETF filing drops Coinbase as custodian, names Anchorage
description: Grayscale submitted an amended S-1 filing with the SEC for its proposed Hyperliquid ETF (ticker: GHYP), replacing Coinbase with Anchorage Digital Bank as the fund's custodian — a notable counterparty shift given Coinbase Custody Trust Company's dominant position across nearly all existing U.S. spot crypto ETFs. Anchorage, the first federally chartered crypto bank in the U.S. and recently valued at $4.2 billion following a $100 million Tether equity investment, had previously served only as a secondary custodian for Grayscale's Bitcoin and Ethereum trusts, making this a meaningful elevation of the relationship.

initiative: A year under Paul Atkins: SEC crypto embrace grows as prediction markets draw scrutiny
description: Nearly one year into his tenure, SEC Chair Paul Atkins publicly reaffirmed the agency's strategic reorientation toward crypto under his "ACT" (Advance, Clarify, Transform) framework, explicitly distancing the SEC from the enforcement-first posture pursued under Gary Gensler—during which the agency pursued numerous actions against major crypto firms on the basis that most digital assets constitute securities, many of which have since been withdrawn. Atkins highlighted "Project Crypto" and an upcoming innovation exemption as concrete regulatory outputs designed to repatriate offshore digital asset activity back to U.S. jurisdiction.

initiative: Tether joins $8 million strategic funding round in RWA firm Kaio
description: "Kaio’s unique position unlocks new pathways for capital formation and investment," Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino said.

initiative: Tether discloses nearly 2 million-share stake in Bitmain-linked Antalpha
description: Nasdaq-listed Antalpha’s lending business is growing quickly despite a more than 27% drop in its stock price.

initiative: Blockchain sleuth accuses RaveDAO of knowing who manipulated the price of its token
description: The on-chain sleuth cited "suspicious" exchange-linked activity as RAVE swung from $60 million in market capitalization to $6 billion and back.

initiative: U.S. crypto adoption is rebounding, bitcoin still dominates, Deutsche Bank says
description: Deutsche Bank published findings from a 3,400-person retail survey across the U.S., U.K., and EU showing U.S. crypto participation rebounded to 12% in March 2026 from a February trough of 7%, recovering to levels last seen in mid-2025, though the survey's all-time high remains just 14% since tracking began in 2023. The recovery coincided with approximately $1.3 billion in net inflows into U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs during March, suggesting institutional and retail demand are moving in parallel after a soft start to the year. Despite the participation rebound, consumer price sentiment is bearish, with a majority of U.S. respondents expecting bitcoin to trade below current ~$75,000 levels by year-end and only 3% anticipating a return to the late-2025 peak above $120,000.

initiative: Ripple races to make XRP Ledger quantum-ready as computing threat turns ‘credible’
description: The company also made the case that XRP Ledger is better prepared than other blockchains, including Ethereum.

initiative: Piero Cipollone: Sparking the transformation of finance - tokenisation and the role of central banks
description: ECB Executive Board member Piero Cipollone used a Harvard Law School symposium on 15 April 2026 to lay out the institutional case for why tokenisation and distributed ledger technology represent a structurally distinct break from prior waves of financial innovation, rather than incremental improvement. His central empirical anchor was the stubborn persistence of financial intermediation costs — roughly 2% of intermediated assets in the United States since the late 19th century, a figure he noted holds across major European economies — arguing that derivatives, electronic trading, and dematerialisation all failed to pass efficiency gains through to end borrowers and savers.

initiative: Vasileios Madouros: Minding the tails - safeguarding resilience of non-bank finance
description: Vasileios Madouros, Deputy Governor of the Central Bank of Ireland, delivered a policy speech to Irish Funds in Dublin on 9 April 2026, setting out the central bank's supervisory posture toward the non-bank financial intermediation (NBFI) sector amid an elevated tail-risk environment. He framed the speech around the structural weight of non-banks — which now hold roughly half of global financial assets — and Ireland's outsized role as a domicile for European fund activity, with approximately €6 trillion in assets under management.

initiative: Dimitar Radev: The current environment is not defined primarily by the baseline scenario. It is defined by the widening range of outcomes around it
description: Bulgarian National Bank Governor Dimitar Radev delivered a formal macroeconomic assessment to diplomatic mission heads in Sofia on 30 March 2026, framing the current conjuncture not as a deviation from a baseline but as a structural widening of the distribution of plausible outcomes — a distinction with direct implications for how central banks and fiscal authorities calibrate policy buffers. Radev disclosed that the ECB Governing Council's March 18–19 meeting specifically modelled Middle East conflict transmission into euro area inflation via energy prices, concluding that short-term effects are manageable but medium-term persistence remains contingent on conflict duration and second-round pass-through.

initiative: Global stablecoin rulemaking slows, prompting BIS to urge cooperation to avoid fragmentation risks
description: To mitigate risks like sudden withdrawals, policymakers are debating safeguards such as limiting interest payments and offering issuers access to central bank backstops.

initiative: Tether backs UAE tokenization firm KAIO in $8M funding round to bring Emirati funds onchain
description: The Abu Dhabi regulated firm build infrastruture to distribute insitutional funds on blockchain rails, lowering entry barrier for investors.

initiative: Coinbase, Bybit said to be working together on tokenization, custody and distribution of U.S. stocks
description: Coinbase and Bybit are in active discussions to collaborate on the tokenization, custody, and cross-border distribution of U.S. public equities and pre-IPO stocks, with Bybit's extensive retail and institutional reach in Asia serving as the primary distribution channel for U.S.-originated tokenized assets. The arrangement is structured as a product and infrastructure partnership rather than any equity transaction, clarifying earlier market speculation about a potential stake acquisition.

initiative: ESMA publishes latest edition of its newsletter
description: Key publications featured in this edition include: Annual transparency calculations for equity and equity-like instruments ; a joint EBA-ESMA consultation on revised suitability assessment requirements for banks and investment firms ; ESMA’s proposals to simplify MiFID II/MiFIR obligations on market data ; and Statement supporting the smooth implementation of the Listing Act and simplified prospectus compliance for issuers . Other updates in this edition cover new Q&As , EMIR 3, supervisory and enforcement actions, market abuse guidelines , sustainability reporting , and upcoming events.

initiative: The geography of AI firms
description: BIS Working Paper: Mapping the Global AI Production Landscape Published by the Bank for International Settlements (BIS Working Paper No. 1343), this research constructs a novel dataset identifying 1,246 AI-producing firms across 32 economies, categorised across five supply chain layers spanning compute infrastructure through to end-user applications. The US and China dominate production with roughly 700 and 250 firms respectively, while most other economies show narrow specialisation—predominantly in compute—and strong home bias in cross-border investment flows.

initiative: BIS says stablecoins act more like ETFs than money, warns of fragmentation without global rules: report
description: The BIS warned stablecoins resemble ETFs, not money, and called for global rules to prevent fragmentation across the $300 billion market.

initiative: BoE governor urges quick action on global stablecoin standards
description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey calls for global standards on stablecoins to ensure stable value and enhance regulatory consistency.

initiative: Aave’s TVL Tanks $6.6 Billion as Kelp DAO Hack Sparks Bad Debt and Structural Fears
description: After the Kelp DAO exploit, the attacker used $292 million in stolen rsETH as collateral on Aave V3, generating roughly $196 million in bad debt and triggering a $6.6 billion TVL collapse.

initiative: Lydian launches Visa crypto card
description: Lydian, a digital assets payment infrastructure provider backed by Tether and Cantor Fitzgerald, is launching a co-branded Visa Platinum card issued by Rain.

initiative: Spot bitcoin ETFs log nearly $1 billion in weekly inflows, highest since mid-January
description: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs collectively attracted $996.4 million in net inflows during the week ending April 20, 2026, the strongest weekly figure since mid-January and the third consecutive week of positive flows, bringing the three-week cumulative total above $1.8 billion. BlackRock's IBIT dominated activity, accounting for $906 million of the weekly total, consistent with its position as the largest bitcoin ETF by net assets. Morgan Stanley's recently launched MSBT — which debuted April 8 and is notable as a wirehoused, advisor-distributed product rather than a direct retail vehicle — completed its first full trading week with $71 million in net inflows and no daily outflow sessions since launch.

initiative: Bank of France calls for ‘strengthening of MiCA’ on stablecoins
description: Bank of France Deputy Governor Denis Beau is advocating for stricter MiCA regulations on stablecoins to mitigate risks in the EU payment system.

initiative: Polymarket seeks $400 million raise at $15 billion valuation: report
description: Polymarket had a $9 billion post-money valuation in October after Intercontinental Exchange agreed to invest up to $2 billion.

initiative: Kraken parent to pay $550m for CFTC-licensed derivatives platform Bitnomial
description: The parent company of crypto exchange Kraken has struck a $550 million deal to buy Bitnomial, a fully CFTC-licensed derivatives platform built for digital assets.

initiative: Aave records $6 billion TVL drop as Kelp hack exposes structural risk at DeFi lender
description: The AAVE token fell 16% and deposits fled the protocol after attackers used drained rsETH as collateral to borrow wrapped ether, leaving Aave to quantify how much bad debt it is now carrying.

initiative: Nomura study says 65% of institutional investors see crypto as a vital portfolio diversifier
description: Nomura and its crypto subsidiary Laser Digital surveyed more than 500 investment professionals in Japan, finding a measurable improvement in institutional sentiment toward digital assets compared to their 2024 baseline. The shift is being underpinned by Japan's ongoing refinement of its crypto regulatory framework — covering asset classification, taxation, and investor protections — alongside the broader global expansion of regulated investment vehicles such as ETFs and tokenized funds, which have lowered the structural barriers to entry.

initiative: Stripe doubles down on blockchain and stablecoins, aiming to become 'AWS for money'
description: Demand is emerging fastest in the Global South and cross-border use cases, where cards fail and currencies are unstable, said Adrien Duchâteau, Stripe's crypto GTM lead.

initiative: Zondacrypto under fire as Poland's prime minister links exchange to legislative interference
description: Polish cryptocurrency exchange Zondacrypto is facing simultaneous operational, political, and regulatory crises following reports of frozen or delayed customer withdrawals since late March. Prime Minister Donald Tusk told parliament the exchange had financially backed legislators who blocked EU crypto market regulation, and separately flagged alleged links to Russia, raising the political stakes for an already embattled platform. CEO Przemysław Kral acknowledged the exchange cannot access a wallet holding approximately 4,500 BTC (~$330 million) because former CEO Sylwester Suszek—who has been missing for four years—failed to transfer the private key during the 2021 ownership transition from BitBay, undermining the wallet's utility as a proof-of-reserves.

initiative: Wrapped XRP goes live on Solana, broadening DeFi access for Ripple-linked token
description: Wrapped XRP on Solana lets XRP holders access Jupiter, Phantom, and Meteora without selling the asset, the latest step in Hex Trust's multi-chain wXRP rollout first announced in December.

initiative: NYSE files first SEC rule change on tokenization journey
description: NYSE has submitted a formal rule change filing to the SEC seeking authorization to trade tokenized securities on its primary order book, following the same structural framework established by Nasdaq's March 2026 filing and the DTC's December 2025 no-action letter, which together created the regulatory scaffolding for tokenized equity entitlements within existing market infrastructure. The proposal covers Russell 1000 constituents and major index ETFs, with tokenized and conventional shares sharing the same CUSIP, execution priority, and T+1 settlement pathway through NSCC/DTC rails — meaning no parallel settlement system is required.

initiative: Sanctioned Russia-Linked Exchange Grinex Suspends Operations Following Alleged Cyberattack
description: Government Unveils Sweeping Enforcement Actions Against Southeast Asian Scam Centers and Crypto Fraud Networks April 24, 2026 Crime EU’s 20th Russia Sanctions Package Signals a New Era of Crypto-Specific Enforcement April 24, 2026 Subscribe to our weekly newsletter Products Reactor Wallet Scan Rapid KYT VASP Risking Address Screening Sentinel Alterya Hexagate Chainalysis Data Solutions (DS) Industries Law Enforcement Centralized Exchanges Financial Institutions Tax Agencies Regulators Stablecoin Risk Management Decentralized Finance Consumer Brands Cybersecurity Join Us Careers Open Positions Company About Us Why Chainalysis Partners Media Resources Scam Prevention Global Español Português Resources Blog Webinars Podcast Reports Services Global Services Training & Certification Security &

initiative: Tabby secures UAE licence
description: Tabby has been granted a Stored Value Facilities (SVF) licence by the Central Bank of the UAE (CBUAE), authorising the company to hold customer funds and introduce a new suite of financial products, including spending accounts, cards and money management tools.

initiative: Congresswoman Biggs discloses six-figure BlackRock bitcoin ETF buy as BTC rebounds
description: U.S. Representative Sheri Biggs filed a House periodic transaction report disclosing that her spouse purchased between $100,001 and $250,000 of BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) on March 4, 2026, through a professionally managed UBS account held under the W.S.B Trust. The disclosure was submitted on April 16—43 days after the trade—placing it within the STOCK Act's mandatory 45-day reporting window, a meaningful distinction from a prior IBIT purchase by Biggs that was filed late and drew congressional trading transparency criticism. The March 4 entry point coincided with a volatile session in which bitcoin moved from roughly $68,000 to above $72,000, and the position has since appreciated as BTC approached $78,000 by mid-April.

initiative: Circle rolls out USDC Bridge for native cross-chain stablecoin transfers
description: USDC Bridge is Circle’s official, native cross-chain bridging solution powered by its Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol.

initiative: French minister urges banks to expand euro stablecoins, tokenized deposits: report
description: French minister urges European banks to expand euro stablecoins and tokenized deposits as dollar-pegged supply tops $300 billion.

initiative: Moody’s agentic workflows land on AWS Marketplace
description: Moody’s Corporation (NYSE: MCO) today announced that its Moody’s Agentic Solutions (MAS) workflows are now available in AWS Marketplace.

initiative: Kraken parent Payward agrees to acquire Bitnomial for up to $550 million
description: Payward is acquiring Chicago-based Bitnomial in a cash-and-stock deal worth up to $550 million, contingent on CFTC approval and expected to close in the first half of 2026. The strategic rationale centers on Bitnomial's rare trifecta of CFTC licenses — a designated contract market, a derivatives clearing organization, and a futures commission merchant — a regulatory infrastructure that took over a decade to assemble and would be prohibitively difficult to replicate organically. The acquisition gives Kraken a compliant domestic venue to launch spot margin, perpetuals, and options under U.S. oversight, and underpins a B2B initiative through Payward Services that will allow banks, fintechs, and brokerages to access regulated derivatives via a single integration.

initiative: France's finance minister calls for more euro stablecoins in sign of government policy shift
description: The statements signal a potential shift in stance within the French government and its central bank.

initiative: Ebanx expands into five new countries
description: Ebanx, a global technology company specialising in cross-border payment services for emerging markets, will begin operating in five new countries: Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam, and Turkey.

initiative: Kraken's parent company Payward to acquire derivatives exchange Bitnomial for $550 million in cash and stock
description: Payward is acquiring Bitnomial, a Chicago-based derivatives platform founded over a decade ago that holds the full trifecta of U.S. regulatory licenses — a designated contract market, a derivatives clearing organization, and a futures commission merchant registration with the CFTC — making it the only crypto-native firm to have assembled that complete stack. Rather than pursuing those approvals organically, Payward is paying up to $550 million in cash and stock to absorb the infrastructure directly, folding it into an expanding derivatives ecosystem that already includes the $1.5 billion NinjaTrader acquisition from 2025. The combined entity will offer spot margin, perpetual futures, and options to U.S.

initiative: Drift Secures $147.5 Million From Tether, Drops Circle’s USDC After Massive Exploit
description: Following the April 1 exploit in which North Korean state-sponsored hackers exploited a Solana pre-signing mechanism to seize governance control and drain approximately $295 million in user funds, Drift Protocol has secured a $147.5 million recovery facility anchored by up to $127.5 million from Tether, with the remainder from undisclosed partners, structured as a revenue-linked credit facility to gradually repay affected users. As part of the relaunch, Drift is replacing Circle's USDC with Tether's USDT as its primary settlement asset on Solana — a direct operational consequence of Circle's refusal to freeze roughly $232 million in stolen USDC that attackers bridged to Ethereum across more than 100 transactions over several hours using Circle's own cross-chain transfer protocol.

initiative: Tillis Delays Clarity Act Stablecoin Yield Text, Citing Uncertainty Over Markup Timing
description: Sen. Thom Tillis said he won't release the stablecoin yield compromise text this week, pushing back a key step in the months-long fight between banks and crypto firms over the CLARITY Act.

initiative: Charles Schwab Launches Spot Bitcoin and Ethereum Trading in Phased Rollout
description: The brokerage giant begins rolling out Schwab Crypto to its 39 million clients, pricing trades at 75 basis points with Paxos handling custody.

initiative: Tether launches stablecoin wallet
description: Tether launched a stablecoin wallet on Tuesday, allowing users to transfer digital dollars, tokenised gold, and bitcoin across multiple blockchains.

initiative: Russia-linked Grinex exchange halts operations after $13 million ‘state-backed’ hack
description: Grinex — the Kyrgyzstan-based exchange that rebranded from Garantex following coordinated 2025 sanctions imposed by the U.S., U.K., and EU — suspended all trading and client withdrawals after attackers drained approximately 1 billion rubles (~$13 million), predominantly in USDT held on the Tron blockchain across 54 identified wallet addresses. The platform publicly attributed the breach to state-sponsored actors from "unfriendly states," framing it as a deliberate strike against Russian financial infrastructure, though no independent attribution has been confirmed.

initiative: AI will control your payments sooner than you think - Bunita Sawhney (CPO, Mastercard)
description: In this episode of the WRAP UP Podcast, I sat down with Bunita Sawhney, Chief Consumer Product Officer at Mastercard, to reflect on how consumer payments are changing beneath the surface.

initiative: UK lawmaker urges FCA probe of Farage crypto promotion
description: Liberal Democrat Deputy Leader Daisy Cooper formally wrote to FCA CEO Nikhil Rathi requesting an investigation into Nigel Farage's participation in a promotional video for Aquis-listed Stack BTC, in which the company announced Farage had executed a £2 million Bitcoin purchase on its behalf — weeks after he had personally invested £215,000 in the firm. Cooper's complaint centers on potential market abuse under UK market manipulation rules, conflict of interest, and consumer harm, drawing a parallel to Bloomberg's findings that Trump and associates extracted $1.4 billion from crypto ventures while shaping favorable regulation.

initiative: Hacked DeFi platform Drift gets funds to repay customers and relaunch with Tether
description: Drift, the DeFi platform that lost around $270 million in clients assets to a North Korean hack earlier this month, has secured $147.5 million in funding from Tether and partners to recover user funds and relaunch with the Tether stablecoin at its centre.

initiative: Circle hit with class action lawsuit over alleged inaction in $280 million Drift exploit
description: Law firm Gibbs Mura filed a class action suit against Circle Internet Financial on behalf of Drift Protocol investors who suffered losses in the April 1 exploit, which saw approximately $280 million drained from the Solana-based decentralized exchange after an attacker introduced a malicious asset and removed withdrawal limits following a six-month infiltration. The plaintiffs allege Circle had both the technical capability and contractual authority to freeze the stolen USDC but failed to act during a roughly six-hour window in which over $230 million was bridged from Solana to Ethereum via Circle's own cross-chain transfer protocol.

initiative: ESAs spring risk update highlights geopolitical pressures and rising private finance risks
description: The three European Supervisory Authorities — EBA, EIOPA, and ESMA — published their Joint Committee spring 2026 risk update on 27 March, identifying Middle East conflict, private credit opacity, and geopolitical cyber exposure as the primary stress vectors for EU financial markets. On the private finance side, the ESAs flagged that limited data availability, low transparency, and complex interconnections between private credit funds and the broader system create conditions for abrupt liquidity shifts and contagion, citing recent stress in US private credit vehicles exposed to AI-driven disruption of legacy software borrowers as a live illustration.

initiative: Postponement of the rollout for Commodity Derivatives Weekly Position Reporting
description: ESMA identified technical deficiencies during the final pre-launch testing of its new Commodity Derivatives Weekly Position Reporting infrastructure, forcing a delay beyond the previously communicated 1 April 2026 go-live date. The issues centre on system stability and data quality, requiring additional corrective work and full validation before the platform can be safely deployed. Reporting entities subject to MiFID II commodity derivatives position reporting obligations must continue submitting data through the existing solution until a revised launch date is confirmed. The delay introduces operational uncertainty for trading venues and investment firms that had prepared internal workflows around the original cutover timeline.

initiative: Russia-linked Grinex exchange suspends operations after $15 million hack, blames ‘hostile states’
description: Liquidity and users of the sanctioned Garantex exchange have flowed into Grinex over the past year, Elliptic claims.

initiative: Morgan Stanley’s Amy Oldenburg says crypto ‘becoming daily business’ across the firm
description: Morgan Stanley's head of digital asset strategy Amy Oldenburg, who assumed the newly created role in February 2026, described crypto as transitioning from a peripheral initiative into core daily operations spanning the firm's institutional, wealth, and asset management divisions. The bank is actively auditing its internal infrastructure—wallet custody, data feeds, compliance workflows, and transaction settlement rails—to identify where blockchain-based processes can be embedded into existing systems rather than run in parallel. A key bottleneck is the absence of fully settled digital cash rails for tokenized assets, a gap Oldenburg tied directly to incomplete regulatory frameworks around stablecoins and tokenization that have yet to be finalized.

initiative: U.S. CFTC's Selig says AI has helped make up for staffing cuts at key crypto watchdog
description: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig testified before the House Agriculture Committee that the agency has deployed AI tools — including Microsoft Copilot — to absorb productivity losses stemming from a roughly 25% reduction in headcount since 2025, even as its regulatory mandate has expanded materially into spot crypto markets and prediction markets. The enforcement division now stands at approximately 108 staff, about 23% below its 2025 level of 140, despite a budget request that adds only three additional enforcement positions. Selig confirmed that "numerous investigations" are underway in prediction markets — where platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi have scaled from millions to billions in volume and face allegations of insider trading tied to U.S.

initiative: Elon Musk’s X Money stands to disrupt payments and pressure PayPal, but crypto plans may hit regulatory hurdles: Mizuho
description: X launched a new feature this week where users can view financial data on stocks and cryptocurrencies directly.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with Community Bankshares, Inc.
description: Federal Reserve Issues Cease and Desist Order Against Community Bankshares, Inc. The Federal Reserve Board executed a formal Cease and Desist Order dated April 14, 2026 against Community Bankshares, Inc., a bank holding company headquartered in LaGrange, Georgia, with the action publicly announced two days later. A Cease and Desist Order is among the more serious enforcement tools available to the Fed, legally compelling the institution to halt specific unsafe or unsound practices and requiring affirmative corrective measures under threat of further penalty.

initiative: SEC seeks public comment on consolidated audit trail and other data sources
description: The Securities and Exchange Commission today issued a concept release soliciting public comment in support of a comprehensive review of the Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT) and other audit trails and related data sources currently used in the regulation of U.S. securities markets.

initiative: Alkami unveils AI Code Studio
description: Alkami Technology, Inc. (Nasdaq: ALKT) ("Alkami"), a leading digital sales and service platform provider for financial institutions in the U.S., today announced the introduction of Alkami Code Studio, a new artificial intelligence (AI)-powered development capability that underscores the company's continued investment in generative AI and open architecture as part of its Digital Sales & Service Platform strategy.

initiative: The 24-hour trap: Why the UK’s new crypto rules could catch some firms off guard
description: The UK Financial Conduct Authority published its Cryptoasset Perimeter Guidance on April 16, drawing most crypto-related activities under the Financial Services and Markets Act framework ahead of an October 2027 implementation deadline. A critical operational threshold is the 24-hour custody rule: any firm retaining client assets beyond that window during settlement, or holding technical authority to override client control, is classified as a regulated custodian and must obtain a full safeguarding licence — a designation that could unexpectedly capture software providers, wallets, and trading platforms that have not previously considered themselves custody businesses.

initiative: AI agents make card purchases with new Mastercard–Lobster.cash integration
description: Payments companies are increasingly investing in new tools that will enable agents to safely execute transactions.

initiative: Drift gets $148 million funding from Tether and partners as it replaces Circle stablecoin with USDT after massive exploit
description: The money will be used by Drift to recover user funds after more than $270 million in clients assets were exploited this month, and will relaunch the protocol as a USDT-based perpetuals DEX on Solana.

initiative: BackBase taps Ninth Wave to provide a bridge between banks and client ERP systems
description: Backbase, the leader in AI-native banking technology, and Ninth Wave, a leading provider of secure Open Finance connectivity, announced a strategic partnership today that will enable banks to offer a seamless connection between their business clients’ ERP and accounting systems and their banking data.

initiative: MUFG expands relationship with Finastra to power US ACH payments
description: Finastra, a global leader in financial services software, today announced that MUFG (Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group), Japan’s largest bank, has selected Global PAYplus to support ACH services in the United States.

initiative: Bitcoin ETFs add $186M as Morgan Stanley’s MSBT inflows top $100M in first six trading days
description: On April 16, 2026, U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs collectively attracted $186.1 million in net inflows for the session, with BlackRock's IBIT accounting for the bulk at $291.9 million while offsetting redemptions from Fidelity's FBTC, ARK/21Shares' ARKB, and Grayscale's GBTC. The session brought cumulative net inflows across the 13-fund cohort to approximately $57.1 billion since the January 2024 launch, with total net assets representing 6.5% of bitcoin's market capitalization. Morgan Stanley's MSBT, which launched under the firm's registered investment adviser and broker-dealer distribution infrastructure, crossed $103 million in cumulative net inflows within six trading days—surpassing the entire lifetime total of WisdomTree's BTCW, a fund that has operated considerably longer.

initiative: Drift secures up to $127 million from Tether for user recovery, pivots from Circle’s USDC to USDT after $280 million exploit
description: Following its April 1 exploit — initially disclosed at $200 million in losses and later revised to approximately $280–296 million — Solana-based perpetuals platform Drift Protocol has arranged a recovery package of up to $127.5 million anchored by Tether, structured as a $100 million revenue-linked credit facility supplemented by ecosystem grants and market-maker loans directed into a dedicated user recovery pool. The protocol will issue a separate, transferable recovery token to affected users representing a proportional claim on that pool, allowing liquidity access ahead of full repayment as exchange revenues accrue and any forensically recovered assets are repatriated.

initiative: ESMA launches a call for evidence on restricted subscription and private credit ratings
description: ESMA Call for Evidence: Restricted Subscription and Private Credit Ratings ESMA has opened a formal call for evidence seeking market input on restricted subscription and private credit ratings — products where access to the rating output is limited to a defined set of contracting parties rather than disclosed publicly. The initiative is directed at understanding use cases, counterparty characteristics, and whether the analytical rigour and governance controls applied to these non-public ratings are meaningfully comparable to those governing publicly disclosed ratings under the CRA Regulation.

initiative: UK FCA seeks fresh feedback on crypto rules ahead of October 2027 rollout
description: Ahead of a planned implementation in 2027, the FCA is seeking feedback on stablecoins, trading platforms, custody, and staking.

initiative: Cyber risk stress testing for banks
description: The Bank for International Settlements' Financial Stability Institute published a policy brief cataloguing how supervisory authorities are designing and deploying cyber stress tests against banks, identifying two distinct methodological frameworks: firm-focused tests, which assess individual institution resilience and recovery capacity, and system-focused tests, which evaluate sector-wide contagion and financial stability implications. The brief draws on recent supervisory exercises across multiple jurisdictions to analyse how authorities select between these approaches depending on their institutional mandates and oversight objectives.

initiative: ABN Amro opens access to crypto investments
description: ABN Amro is to give customers indirect exposure to cryptocurrencies through the introduction Crypto Exchange Traded Products (ETPs) and Capital Protected Notes (CPNs)

initiative: CaixaBank collaborates with Flanks to give wealth clients aggregated view of financial assets
description: CaixaBank has launched GlobalView, its global financial positions aggregator, which enables the bank’s Wealth Management clients—the entity’s private banking division—to consolidate and analyse all their financial positions across multiple financial institutions under a single, unified framework.

initiative: Michael S Barr: Rural communities - worth the investment
description: Federal Reserve Governor Highlights Rural Capital Access and CDFI Role Speaking at a Washington forum focused on rural investment, Fed Governor Michael Barr drew on field visits to underserved communities — including the Mississippi Delta — to underscore persistent gaps in rural financial access and the institutional responses emerging to address them. He cited a concrete example of a struggling community bank donating its branch building to a CDFI to preserve local financial services, and pointed to New Markets Tax Credits as an effective tool in catalyzing private investment in distressed areas. The remarks signal continued Fed-level attention to community reinvestment frameworks, CDFI capacity, and tax credit mechanisms as policy-relevant instruments for rural economic inclusion.

initiative: Bas ter Weel: Resilient payments - cash, card, connected
description: Opening address by Mr Bas ter Weel, Executive Board Member of Monetary Affairs of De Nederlandsche Bank, at the DNB's Retail Payments Research Conference 2026 "Future-proof payments: innovative, resilient and inclusive", Amsterdam, 9 April 2026.

initiative: Richard Byles: Redefining economic leadership - building resilient financial ecosystems in emerging markets
description: Bank of Jamaica Governor Richard Byles addressed Caribbean finance executives at the Caribbean CFO Summit 2026 in Kingston, setting out the central bank's regulatory philosophy and the concrete policy measures underpinning Jamaica's financial stability framework. He disclosed that Jamaica holds a record US$6.8 billion in foreign reserves and confirmed that the central bank has deliberately adopted a calibrated, proportionate adaptation of Basel III standards rather than mechanical full implementation, prioritising liquidity resilience and capital quality improvements suited to the domestic system's structure.

initiative: The cheapest bitcoin ETF yet: Morgan Stanley uses 0.14% fee to draw $100 million in first week
description: Morgan Stanley's spot bitcoin ETF (ticker: MSBT), which began trading April 8, accumulated over $100 million in its first week by undercutting all existing spot bitcoin ETF competitors with a 0.14% expense ratio and leveraging the firm's wealth management distribution network, which spans trillions in client assets and gives financial advisors a direct channel to route client allocations into the product. The fund benchmarks to the CoinDesk Bitcoin Benchmark 4 PM New York Settlement Rate and has been described internally as the firm's most successful ETF launch to date, though it remains dwarfed by BlackRock's IBIT at over $53 billion in AUM.

initiative: Circle CEO says China could launch yuan stablecoin within 3 to 5 years as currency race heats up
description: The pitch is global scale for the yuan, but capital controls, offshore limits and convertibility gaps still stand in the way

initiative: The CLARITY Act breakthrough: Why JPMorgan says the U.S. crypto rulebook is ‘close to completion’
description: Analysts at the bank say negotiations for the CLARITY Act are reaching a final breakthrough as lawmakers resolve long-standing disputes over stablecoin rewards and agency oversight.

initiative: Dimitar Radev: Economic prospects in an uncertain global environment
description: Bulgarian National Bank Governor Dimitar Radev, speaking at the AmCham Business Breakfast in Sofia on 3 April 2026, laid out the BNB's revised macroeconomic outlook amid energy market disruption stemming from the Middle East conflict, projecting Bulgarian GDP growth of approximately 3.0% for 2026 against an inflation rise to roughly 3.7%, with the bank's internal scenario analysis indicating conditions are tracking closer to its "adverse" rather than baseline path. Radev flagged that reduced fiscal headroom—following several years of drawing down pre-pandemic buffers—limits the state's capacity for broad support measures, shifting a greater share of adjustment onto the private sector in terms of cost management, liquidity discipline, and selective capital allocation.

initiative: CPMI-IOSCO assesses that the United Kingdom has implemented the Principles for financial market infrastructures for two FMI types, but recommends some improvements
description: The UK's framework for systemically important payment systems and central securities depositories/securities settlement systems is complete and consistent with the CPMI-IOSCO Principles for financial market infrastructures (PFMI) in most aspects. The assessment reflects the status of implementation as of September 2023.

initiative: Euronet acquires PaynoPain
description: Euronet Worldwide (NASDAQ: EEFT), a global leader in payment processing and cross-border transactions, today announced it has entered into an agreement to acquire PaynoPain, a Spanish fintech company recognized for its digital-first, online payment solutions serving businesses of all sizes—from SMEs to large enterprises—across industries including e-commerce, hospitality, microfinance, and marketplace platforms.

initiative: Monavate integrates with Sumsub for identity verification
description: Sumsub, a global verification and anti-fraud leader trusted by 4,000 businesses, has partnered with Monavate, a regulated global payments infrastructure provider with principal memberships across Visa, Mastercard, and Discover.

initiative: Circle CEO Allaire sees ‘tremendous opportunity’ for yuan stablecoin: report
description: Chinese authorities have said yuan-pegged stablecoins cannot be issued offshore without prior regulatory approval.

initiative: Piero Cipollone: The digital euro in a fragmenting world - ensuring Europe's resilience and autonomy in payments
description: Public lecture by Mr Piero Cipollone, Member of the Executive Board of the European Central Bank, at an event hosted by the Stockholm School of Economics in Riga and Latvijas Banka, Riga, 1 April 2026.

initiative: Ripple partners with Korea's Kyobo Life to tokenize government bond settlement
description: The deal is Ripple's first with a Korean insurer and targets near real-time settlement of Korean treasuries, though the release stops short of committing to a live volume or timeline.

initiative: Thailand eyes stricter checks on crypto firm backers
description: Thailand SEC Proposes Expanded Major Shareholder Definition for Crypto and Securities Firms Thailand's Securities and Exchange Commission has opened a public consultation, closing April 22, on draft rules that would classify funding providers and financial backers of existing major shareholders as major shareholders themselves, thereby subjecting them to the same formal SEC approval requirements. The proposal follows March 2026 rule changes that tightened indirect ownership tracing through corporate chains and expanded the definition of controlling persons to include spouses, cohabitants, minor children, and parties acting in concert on voting rights, which took effect March 4 with a 180-day compliance window for operators to submit approval requests.

initiative: Italy's UniCredit invests €4 million in BlockInvest
description: Italy's UniCredit has invested €4 million to acquire a 16% stake in BlockInvest, furthering its ambitions to export tokenized financial assets across the bank's main markets

initiative: DTCC makes public cloud move with AWS and Microsoft
description: The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC) is working with AWS and Microsoft to migrate its core market and digital market infrastructure to the public cloud.

initiative: SocGen brings stablecoin to MetaMask wallet users
description: Societe Generale's digital asset unit SG-Forge is joining forces with blockchain outfit Consensys to expand global access to its USD CoinVertible stablecoin through integration with the MetaMask Web3 wallet.

initiative: Payabl. launches Click to pay with Visa
description: payabl. has launched Click to pay with Visa, a new card payment experience designed to help merchants reduce checkout friction, improve authorisation rates, and deliver a faster, more secure online payment journey.

initiative: PayPal Adds Pix to the Mix
description: Hey Payments Fanatic! PayPal is bringing Pix, the instant payment method that has become Brazil's financial backbone with over 170 million users, to its SMB merchant base. With Pix now available through PayPal Complete Payments (PPCP), businesses can offer Brazil’s most widely used payment method directly

initiative: Bitcoin capped at $75,000 as Nasdaq and S&P 500 fly to new record highs
description: Bitcoin has been unable to sustain a break above the $75,000 level, with the most recent attempt on April 15 stalling at the same technical threshold that has capped price action for roughly two months, leaving the asset approximately 40% below its October 2025 peak. The divergence with U.S. equities is operationally notable: the Nasdaq closed above 24,000 for its 11th consecutive record session and the S&P 500 cleared 7,000, suggesting macro risk appetite is not the binding constraint on bitcoin's underperformance. Crypto-adjacent equities—Coinbase (+6.2%), Robinhood (+10%), and Strategy (+4.4%)—participated in the broader rally, indicating that institutional exposure is rotating into listed proxies rather than spot BTC.

initiative: CryptoQuant says bitcoin rally faces rising profit-taking risk as exchange inflows jump
description: CryptoQuant's head of research, Julio Moreno, published a report flagging a material deterioration in Bitcoin's short-term demand structure as the asset briefly reclaimed $76,000 for the first time since early February. Hourly exchange inflows surged to approximately 11,000 BTC — the highest since late December 2025 — with large individual deposits to Binance exceeding 1,000 BTC pushing the mean deposit size to 2.25 BTC, the highest daily reading since July 2024.

initiative: Former Lutnick-run Cantor Fitzgerald donates $10 million to crypto PAC led by Tether exec
description: Cantor Fitzgerald, now controlled by Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick's sons following his move into the Cabinet, made a $10 million contribution in February to the Fellowship PAC via a Federal Election Commission-disclosed filing — deepening the firm's already well-documented entanglement with the crypto industry. The PAC, which launched in September 2025 with over $100 million in committed backing, is chaired by Tether U.S. executive Jesse Spiro and counts Anchorage Digital as an additional donor at $1 million. The contribution is operationally significant because Cantor, Tether, and Anchorage form the core institutional infrastructure behind USAT, Tether's U.S.-focused stablecoin — with Cantor serving as reserve custodian and preferred primary dealer, and Anchorage acting as issuer.

initiative: Tether backs Stablecoin Development Corporation’s $134 million private placement used to acquire SKY tokens
description: The Stablecoin Development Corporation is a publicly-traded holding company with an approximately 2.15 billion SKY token treasury.

initiative: Crypto's new $11 million PAC booked millions in ads with firm started by Tether US CEO
description: Fellowship PAC, a newly formed crypto-focused super PAC that has raised $11 million to date—$10 million from Cantor Fitzgerald and $1 million from Anchorage Digital—has directed its first $3 million in advertising expenditures to Nxum Group, a firm co-founded by Bo Hines, who serves as CEO of Tether US and previously held a senior White House crypto advisory role under President Trump. The vendor relationship is notable because Tether-affiliated executives chair the PAC and Cantor Fitzgerald—whose former CEO Howard Lutnick now serves as Commerce Secretary—both funds the PAC and custodies Tether's stablecoin reserves, creating a dense web of overlapping institutional relationships subject to scrutiny.

initiative: UK asset manager puts $68 billion of funds on-chain via Calastone token network
description: Legal & General Asset Management brings decades-old money market funds onto blockchain rails to expand access and enable faster settlement.

initiative: Colombian Bre-B Surpasses 600 Million Transactions
description: Hey Payments Fanatic! Colombia’s instant payment revolution has reached a massive milestone as Bre-B concludes its first six months of operations.  Developed by the Central Bank in partnership with ACI Worldwide, the system has already surpassed 600 million transactions and secured over 34 million registered users since

initiative: Why We Submitted Our SEC No-Action Letter Request
description: Ondo Finance has formally petitioned the SEC for a no-action letter to obtain regulatory clarity on whether its tokenized securities products can operate without triggering broker-dealer or transfer agent registration requirements under existing U.S. securities law. The request reflects the firm's effort to establish a compliant pathway for on-chain issuance and secondary transfer of tokenized real-world assets—particularly U.S. Treasury-backed instruments—without relying solely on existing exemptions that were not designed for blockchain settlement mechanics. A favorable staff response would set a meaningful precedent for the broader tokenized securities industry by signaling how the SEC interprets its existing framework when applied to permissioned blockchain infrastructure.

initiative: FSB Chair warns of rising financial risks stemming from Middle East conflict
description: FSB Chair Issues Pre-G20 Warning on Conflict-Driven Systemic Risk In a formal letter addressed to G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors ahead of their April 16 meeting, FSB Chair and Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey outlined how ongoing Middle East conflict is compounding pre-existing structural vulnerabilities across global financial markets. Bailey specifically flagged the risk of simultaneous crystallisation across three interconnected pressure points: leveraged government bond basis trades concentrated among a small number of non-bank funds, stretched asset valuations in sectors including AI, and deteriorating credit quality within opaque private credit markets where contagion risk is amplified by limited transparency.

initiative: Deep Dive: An Analytical Breakdown of Revolut’s 2025 Performance
description: Revolut's full-year 2025 results show £4.5 billion in revenue and £1.7 billion in profit before tax, marking its fifth consecutive profitable year and cementing a $75 billion private valuation — the highest of any European technology company. A structural feature of the results is the revenue composition: fee-based income accounts for 76% of total turnover across six segments, deliberately reducing the interest rate sensitivity that constrains traditional bank earnings, with card payments (22.2%) and subscriptions (15.7%) leading recurring income lines.
